description: TLC is a constitutional runtime that executes multiple, independent epistemic constitutions through one Constitution Interface (TLC-SL). A constitution is a directory of .tlcsl invariants; the runtime (compiler, in-process checker, TLA+ emitter, Policy Engine enforcer) is constitution-agnostic and is not modified to load a new one.

interface.name: TLC-SL Constitution Interface
interface.source: tlc-sl/src/ (compile.mjs, parser.mjs, model.mjs, checker.mjs, interp.mjs, enforce.mjs, targets/)
interface.contract: each invariant declares id, level (BLOCK|WARN|LOG), finite-domain state + per-action inputs, guarded transitions, and a safety predicate; it compiles to runtime enforcement, an exhaustive model check, and a TLA+ export

namespaces.W-*: RECOMMENDED future rename for the Eight Wonders relational-economy ontology (currently labelled I1_..I8_ in the governance-harness probes, which collides with the governance I1-I8 numbering)

constitutions.title: The TLC Governance Constitution
constitutions.domain: sociotechnical runtime governance (Constitution Article VIII + Article VII/X break-glass)

constitutions.title: The Instructional Integrity Constitution
constitutions.domain: instructional systems design (mastery learning, Gagne, Cognitive Load Theory, scaffolding, UDL, Merrill)
